title: Traveling with Kyle - Indonesia Part 1 - Sumatra

transcript.text: Okay next video so yes so we did this canoe trip I think we started pretty
Early in the morning and I ate a bunch of these mushrooms are not nearly as
Strong as Americans though they're really thin and the caps are really
Small like as big as your fingernail and you can eat a whole bunch of them and
They're they're like a tenth as strong as the ones in America but you still trip
I was still tripping and so we can do and this English kid I was with wasn't
Very athletic he was just kind of canoeing he didn't really understand
That you have to dig in if you want to get anywhere but it didn't matter
Because I was doing it and it got kind of windy and at one point I let him do it
But he was totally useless because like we needed one person to pull and another
Person to to actually canoe because it was so windy and we were kind of like in
A shallow part of the lake but we ended up making it to the isthmus that way it
Was it was it was you know so it'd be like halfway around but it wasn't halfway
It was like a lot less than that it was like a hundred miles around the first day
It was like 40 miles the segment was 60 miles and we got to the edge and we
Didn't know what to do from that point because we couldn't carry the boat to the
Other end because it was so heavy and he was like he got kind of frustrated and so
I was I told him okay you can just go ahead and go back take the bus back I'll
Deal with this myself so he left and then I found these Indonesian guys like six of
Them to come with their pickup and hoist that thing on the back of their pickup and
Take it to the other end of it and I paid somebody else to let me put it like in
Their backyard or something and then I went to the hot I ran out ran down to the
Hot springs and stayed at the hot springs hotel and soaked for a little bit and
Slept and then I got up at like six o'clock the next morning and ran back to my
Canoe and got in it and paddled and I think I brought a bunch of water with me a
Couple of water bottles with me and I had a big breakfast and maybe bought brought
Extra food for lunch and stuff and I paddled my brains out and I started six
In the morning and I went around and then I headed north again and it got really
Windy and like the waves got really big and we're kind of like splashing into
The boat and it was really difficult to paddle this thing because this thing
Weighed so much and there was no what do you call it rudder on the bottom of the
Boat like I would paddle I'd have to do like six paddles I think it was at least
Six I had to count them because I had to be very calculated to keep the thing
Going in the direction I needed to go I do six and it would go so it would be
Turning like this six six six six and then I get literally to 45 degrees I'm not
Exaggerating and then I paddle this side six six six six six and then I'd start to
Paddle on this side before it even got straight again and the momentum of it
Just kept it going and I'd keep paddling and until I got to 45 degrees and then
We'd go back and then I'd start paddling over here and I just had a time so the
Boat was doing like this the entire time but it was still kind of going by pretty
Fast because it could still move forward even though it was sideways because it
Didn't have a rudder on it and it got dark and then I saw the light of where I
Was at and I just kept trudging it but like I didn't bring extra enough food or
Something so my stomach shriveled up like I guess because my stomach was
Totally flexed the whole time from from the paddling then it squeezed my
Stomach and all of my acid went up into my throat and my throat was burning hot
Fire it was the most painful experience you could possibly imagine there's
Nothing more painful than that and but you know I had to and even if I drank
Water it didn't really do anything and I think I ran out of water too anyways that
Happened for hours and I think I finally made it back to the it to the town I
Started at at like 1130 at night and I went to my hotel and the person who
Lended who rent me the boat was there wondering where I was and I brought the
Boat because I think I get I brought it back like you know it's supposed to come
Back that day but yeah I left it there and where I got it at the guy's backyard and
Went back to my hotel and hung out for a couple days and he never came back like
Me asking me hey what are you doing come back late but uh yeah then I was there
Like another couple days or something and then I rode my bike back continuing to
It to the place where you take the boat to neon neon so the western side of
Sumatra and I had a really interesting experience I had weed that I bought a
Little bit of weed that I bought there and so I'd ride and then I would uh get
Off the road and smoke and then ride some more bike touring and smoking weed
It's amazing especially if you have music and I think I did have music so
Listen to music smoking weed bike touring and then you get off the road and
Smoke it's awesome because you get to hang out in the woods and get all high in
Nature and then get back on your head but I had to be really careful because the
Roads really narrow and Indonesian drivers are really bad and they don't give you
Any room so I had a mirror on the side of my bike and I and I looked I was
Always looking at every single car and I knew exactly where they were and I
Would I think I would actually get off the road because some of them were we're
Gonna hit me like if I hadn't gotten off the road I would have been hit and
Killed um so I you know you have to be on your toes completely but I got to the
Top of one pass and it was a big downhill and I got off the road I think to have
Like a smoke break or something lunch break and started to rain and I was in
My my t-shirt and my shorts and the rain came down the rain was cold and I was
Freezing shivering but instead of putting on a rain jacket all I had to do is get
On my bike and then I rode down the mountain and the air temperature because I
Was right on the equator warmed me up so I was perfectly warm in a rainstorm
Riding downhill when I was freezing if I was just staying still it was like
Opposite from in Colorado it was pretty cool and then I rode down a beautiful

Movies Malaysia is kind of a constant mountain place the Chinese who control

Nobody said anything I guess that was part of the culture or something I don't

Only like a dollar thing then I rode my bike all the way down to the south side of
Neos where the surfing is and I got a room there I'm on a little my own little

Then I was I actually watched people and I timed with my my stopwatch and it was
35 seconds that this wave carried you so it was really cool big wave that I

Different waves came in and I was riding some other ways which were really big
But you could only serve for a couple seconds but when you were surfing you're
On the wave was so big that you're actually skipping off the water it was
Like it was really cool but Neos is amazing because they had still had the

Lining this big really wide main street that's cobbled and in the middle there's

Little stone about this high that and the kids would run up to it and jump over the

100 years ago they would then their sport was to go to the next village and chop

Very nice actually I would never go back there but I thought I could probably

Indonesians and Moroccans and Brazilians are really full of energy and especially
